title: performance issues with skopeo inspect on large OCI archives
body:
For some reason, skopeo takes a really long time to do an inspect on a large OCI archive, we're talking > 20GB tar files.
It takes up to 2 minutes to just get the metadata, while doing it manually in a script takes milliseconds.

I have not confirmed it yet, but the same thing happens when doing a `skopeo copy` from a large OCI archive to a registry. I'm seeing a massive increase that I cannot explain just based on the zst compression of the blobs.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Just by the number _alone_ (and there is nothing else to go on), I’m not surprised; `skopeo inspect` of a compressed archive is making an uncompressed copy of the whole archive, even if it really needed to read one or two small files.

The general recommendation is to avoid these large files that don’t allow random access, and use a registry. A registry allows random access, cross-image sharing, concurrency, and other features. (Even if it had to be a short-term registry running in a container, with the backing storage a volume.)

If the decompression does not explain it, I wouldn’t be too surprised by inefficiencies in the code when dealing with data at that scale, but then it would probably be up to you to profile the Go code to figure out the exact cause — perhaps using some updated version of (the last commit of) https://github.com/mtrmac/skopeo/tree/profile-options .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if more information becomes available.
